title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: The report examines the use of copyrighted materials, such as music and images, in the creation of AI systems, highlighting the potential tensions between AI development and copyright laws. The assessment aims to provide guidance on how to balance the advancement of AI technology with the protection of intellectual property rights.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Department for Transport (DfT) has created a plan to use artificial intelligence (AI) in the transport sector, aiming to harness its benefits while minimizing potential risks to ensure safer, more efficient, and smarter transport systems. The plan involves collaborating with the transport industry to develop and implement AI technologies that improve services, boost productivity, and enhance customer experience.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report aims to examine how copyright laws apply to the use of existing works in the development of artificial intelligence systems, ensuring that these systems do not infringe on the rights of original creators. The report specifically focuses on Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act, which addresses the use of copyright works in AI development to strike a balance between innovation and intellectual property protection.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary:
Here's a 2-sentence summary:

The government is developing a policy to regulate artificial intelligence (AI) as a medical device, with the aim of ensuring safety, efficacy, and reliability for healthcare purposes. This policy response is based on recommendations from the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) to create a clear and effective framework for AI medical device regulation in the UK.
